SEARCH
Package org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node

Class MapConfigNode<K,V>

java.lang.Object
org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node.ConfigHeaderNode
org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node.ConfigNode<Map<K,V>>
org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node.MapConfigNode<K,V>
Type Parameters:
K - The key type.
V - The value type.
All Implemented Interfaces:
CommentedNode, MapValueNode<K,V>, Node, ValueNode<Map<K,V>>
@AvailableSince("5.7") public class MapConfigNode<K,V> extends ConfigNode<Map<K,V>> implements MapValueNode<K,V>
A config node that contains key-value mappings.
Since:
5.7

    • MapConfigNode

      protected MapConfigNode(@NotNull @NotNull String path, @NotNull @NotNull String[] comments, @Nullable @Nullable String name, @NotNull @NotNull Class<Map<K,V>> type, @NotNull @NotNull String[] aliases, @Nullable @Nullable Supplier<Map<K,V>> defaultValueSupplier, @Nullable @Nullable NodeSuggester suggester, @Nullable @Nullable NodeStringParser<Map<K,V>> stringParser, @Nullable @Nullable NodeSerializer<Map<K,V>> serializer, @Nullable @Nullable Function<Map<K,V>,io.vavr.control.Try<Void>> validator, @Nullable @Nullable NodeValueCallback<Map<K,V>> onLoad, @Nullable @Nullable NodeChangeCallback<Map<K,V>> onLoadAndChange, @Nullable @Nullable NodeChangeCallback<Map<K,V>> onChange, @NotNull @NotNull Class<K> keyType, @NotNull @NotNull Class<V> valueType, @Nullable @Nullable NodeSuggester keySuggester, @Nullable @Nullable NodeSuggester valueSuggester, @Nullable @Nullable NodeStringParser<K> keyStringParser, @Nullable @Nullable NodeStringParser<V> valueStringParser, @Nullable @Nullable NodeSerializer<K> keySerializer, @Nullable @Nullable NodeSerializer<V> valueSerializer, @Nullable @Nullable Function<K,io.vavr.control.Try<Void>> keyValidator, @Nullable @Nullable Function<V,io.vavr.control.Try<Void>> valueValidator)

  • Method Details

    • mapBuilder

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @NotNull public static <K, V> @NotNull MapConfigNode.Builder<K,V> mapBuilder(@NotNull @NotNull String path, @NotNull @NotNull Class<K> keyType, @NotNull @NotNull Class<V> valueType)
      Creates a new builder for a MapConfigNode.
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Parameters:
      path - The path of the node.
      keyType - The map key type.
      valueType - The map value type.
      Returns:
      The new builder.
      Since:
      5.7

    • validateKey

      @NotNull public @NotNull io.vavr.control.Try<Void> validateKey(@Nullable K key)
      Validates a single map key.
      Implementations should return a successful Try when the key is acceptable, or a failed Try when the key is invalid.
      Specified by:
      validateKey in interface MapValueNode<K,V>
      Parameters:
      key - The key to validate, or null if the key is absent.
      Returns:
      A successful Try if the key is valid, or a failed Try if it is not.

    • validateValue

      @NotNull public @NotNull io.vavr.control.Try<Void> validateValue(@Nullable V value)
      Validates a single map value.
      Implementations should return a successful Try when the value is acceptable, or a failed Try when the value is invalid.
      Specified by:
      validateValue in interface MapValueNode<K,V>
      Parameters:
      value - The value to validate, or null if the value is absent.
      Returns:
      A successful Try if the value is valid, or a failed Try if it is not.

    • validateEntry

      @NotNull public @NotNull io.vavr.control.Try<Void> validateEntry(@Nullable K key, @Nullable V value)
      Validates a key-value entry.
      This is typically implemented by composing MapValueNode.validateKey(Object) and MapValueNode.validateValue(Object). If either part fails, the entry is considered invalid.
      Specified by:
      validateEntry in interface MapValueNode<K,V>
      Parameters:
      key - The entry key, or null if the key is absent.
      value - The entry value, or null if the value is absent.
      Returns:
      A successful Try if both parts are valid, or a failed Try if either part is invalid.

    • deserializeEntry

      @NotNull public Map.Entry<K,V> deserializeEntry(@Nullable @Nullable Object key, @Nullable @Nullable Object value)
      Deserializes a raw key-value pair into a map entry.
      This method converts a single persisted entry, not the full map. If either component cannot be converted, implementations may return null for that side of the entry.
      Specified by:
      deserializeEntry in interface MapValueNode<K,V>
      Parameters:
      key - The raw key object, or null if no key is present.
      value - The raw value object, or null if no value is present.
      Returns:
      A map entry containing the deserialized key and value.

    • serializeEntry

      @NotNull public Map.Entry<Object,Object> serializeEntry(@Nullable K key, @Nullable V value)
      Serializes a map entry into raw configuration objects.
      This method converts a single entry to a persisted representation, not the full map. The returned entry contains the serialized key and serialized value.
      Specified by:
      serializeEntry in interface MapValueNode<K,V>
      Parameters:
      key - The entry key, or null if the key is absent.
      value - The entry value, or null if the value is absent.
      Returns:
      A map entry containing the serialized key and value.
